steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to emphasize a difference between the two sculptures. The answer must contrast them — not describe what they share.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — *Don't Cry Over Spilt Milk*, like *Ginger*, is a sculpture.: The word 'like' signals a similarity, not a difference. ✗
  • Choice B — Both De Haan's and Kawauchi's sculptures are crafted from neon lighting.: 'Both' highlights something the two share — not a difference. ✗
  • Choice C — Eve De Haan and Rikako Kawauchi are artists who have used neon in their sculptures.: Again describes a similarity (both use neon). ✗
  • Choice D — Unlike De Haan's sculpture, which spells out a phrase, Kawauchi's is an abstract squiggle shape.: 'Unlike' directly contrasts the two: one spells out words, the other is an abstract form. ✓
That matches Choice D — Unlike De Haan's sculpture, which spells out a phrase, Kawauchi's is an abstract squiggle shape..
